title: Introduction to bridgr
text: Bridge models statistical tools designed address mismatch frequency economic indicators target variable, GDP. instance, GDP typically reported quarterly, many relevant indicators (e.g., industrial production, survey data) available monthly even daily. Bridge models “bridge” gap converting high-frequency indicators form aligns target variable’s frequency. models widely used nowcasting short-term forecasting. particularly useful : Data target variable reported lag (e.g., GDP often released delay). High-frequency indicators provide early signals state economy. leveraging real-time data, bridge models can improve forecast accuracy provide timely insights.

title: Introduction to bridgr
text: Bridge models exploit relationship target variable, quarterly GDP (\(y_t\)), multiple high-frequency indicators (\(x_{t}^{()}\)), observed monthly even higher frequencies. primary challenge harmonize different frequencies preserving information embedded indicators.

title: Introduction to bridgr
text: align high-frequency indicators (\(x_{t}^{()}\)) target variable’s frequency (\(y_t\)), transformation aggregation process applied. Let \(K\) represent number higher-frequency periods within single lower-frequency period (e.g., \(K=3\) monthly data aggregated quarter). aggregation step can represented : \[ \bar{x}_{t}^{()} =  \sum_{k=0}^{K-1} \omega (k) L^{k/K} x_{t}^{()} \] : lag operator defined \(L^{1/3} x_{t}^{()} = x_{t-1/3}^{()}\) \(x_{t}^{()}\): value indicator \(\) period \(t\). \(\bar{x}_{t}^{()}\): aggregated indicator period \(t\). \(\omega (k)\): weight assigned k-th period higher frequency data. many applications, \(\omega (k)\) simply average values higher frequency periods. Alternative aggregation techniques include taking last observation (e.g., last month quarter), applying fixed weighted averages, estimating exponential-Almon weights data. bridgr, supported aggregation choices : "mean" "last" "sum" numeric weights supplied list(...) "expalmon" "expalmon" used one indicator, package estimates weight profiles jointly final bridge-model objective. avoids choosing weighting profile isolation.

title: Introduction to bridgr
text: indicators aligned target frequency, bridge model typically specified linear regression: \[ y_t = \beta_0 + \sum_{ } \sum_{p_i=0}^{P^{()}} \beta_{p_i} L^p \bar{x}_{t}^{()} + \varepsilon_t \] : \(P^{()}\): lags include indicator \(\). \(\beta_{p_i}\): Coefficients capturing relationship indicators target variable. \(\varepsilon_t\): error term. Bridge models also handle cases high-frequency indicators fully observed time forecasting. cases, missing observations current period imputed forecast using time series models (e.g., ARIMA, ETS). allows predictions even recent observations missing. combination aggregation forecasting ensures bridge models versatile tools dealing incomplete data scenarios. package assumes regular frequency ladder second year, default mappings 60 seconds per minute, 7 days per week, 4 weeks per month. defaults can adjusted frequency_conversions. target period contains high-frequency observations implied current mapping, bridgr uses recent observations issues summarized warning. contains fewer observations required, estimation stops error.

title: Introduction to bridgr
text: bridgr package simplifies construction estimation bridge models. vignette demonstrates use package quarterly GDP series (gdp) monthly economic indicator (baro).

title: Introduction to bridgr
text: example, two follwoing Swiss datasets used: gdp: Quarterly GDP data. baro: Monthly economic indicator data.  visualizing data,becomes obvious monthly economic indicator (baro) available higher frequency quarterly GDP data. Moreover, significant correlation.
code: # Load libraries library(bridgr) library(tsbox)  # Example data data("gdp")  # Quarterly GDP data data("baro") # Monthly economic indicator  gdp <- tsbox::ts_na_omit(tsbox::ts_pc(gdp)) # Calculate growth rate  # Visualize the data ts_ggplot(   ts_scale(ts_c(baro, gdp)),   title = "Quarterly gdp and monthly economic indicator",   subtitle = "Scaled to mean 0 and variance 1"   ) +   theme_tsbox()

title: Introduction to bridgr
text: calculating GDP growth rate, one observation less beginning GDP series. bridge() function detects mismatched starting dates aligns earliest common date. model estimated using specified lags target indicator variables. h argument specifies number periods forecast lower frequency variable. fitted object returns target-frequency estimation sample forecast-period regressor set used prediction.
code: # Estimate the bridge model bridge_model <- bridge(   target = gdp,    indic = baro ,    indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "mean",   indic_lags = 1,    target_lags=1,    h=2  ) #> [value]: 'values'  #> [value]: 'values' # Inspect the datasets tail(bridge_model$estimation_set) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   time         gdp  baro baro_lag1 #>   <date>     <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 2021-07-01 2.34  112.      125.  #> 2 2021-10-01 0.411 104.      112.  #> 3 2022-01-01 0.105  97.4     104.  #> 4 2022-04-01 1.03   94.3      97.4 #> 5 2022-07-01 0.255  90.0      94.3 #> 6 2022-10-01 0.102  90.7      90.0 head(bridge_model$forecast_set) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   time        baro baro_lag1 #>   <date>     <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 2023-01-01  97.4      90.7 #> 2 2023-04-01  99.8      97.4

title: Introduction to bridgr
code: # Forecasting using the bridge model fcst <- forecast(bridge_model)  forecast <- data.frame(   "time" = fcst$forecast_set$time,   "forecast" = as.numeric(fcst$mean) )  # Visualize the forecast ts_ggplot(   ts_span(ts_tbl(ts_c(gdp, forecast)), start = 2017),   title = "Forecasted GDP",   subtitle = "Bridge model forecast" ) +   theme_tsbox()

title: Introduction to bridgr
code: # Summarize the information in the bridge model summary(bridge_model) #> Bridge model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp #> Target frequency: quarter (step 1) #> Forecast horizon: 2 #> Formula: gdp ~ baro + baro_lag1 #> ----------------------------------- #> Main model: #> ----------------------------------- #> Series: estimation_xts[, target_name]  #> Regression with ARIMA(1,0,0) errors  #>  #> Coefficients: #>          ar1  intercept    baro  baro_lag1 #>       0.2426    -6.4929  0.1615    -0.0917 #> s.e.  0.1213     1.3430  0.0125     0.0121 #>  #> sigma^2 = 0.5625:  log likelihood = -81.69 #> AIC=173.38   AICc=174.26   BIC=184.9 #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator models: #> ----------------------------------- #> Series: baro #> Frequency: month (step 1) #> Forecast method: auto.arima #> Series: xts_series  #> ARIMA(1,0,2) with non-zero mean  #>  #> Coefficients: #>          ar1     ma1     ma2      mean #>       0.6688  0.5305  0.3316  100.8580 #> s.e.  0.0653  0.0799  0.0753    1.5774 #>  #> sigma^2 = 18.46:  log likelihood = -646.14 #> AIC=1302.28   AICc=1302.55   BIC=1319.36 #> Aggregation: mean #> -----------------------------------

title: Introduction to bridgr
text: want within-period weights estimated data rather fixed simple mean, can use indic_aggregators = "expalmon". optimizer can controlled solver_options. single-indicator models gives data-driven weighting profile high-frequency observations within target period. multi-indicator models, expalmon indicators optimized jointly weighting scheme chosen bridge equation whole.
code: expalmon_model <- bridge(   target = gdp,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "expalmon",   solver_options = list(seed = 123, n_starts = 3),   h = 1 )  summary(expalmon_model) #> Bridge model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp #> Target frequency: quarter (step 1) #> Forecast horizon: 1 #> Formula: gdp ~ baro #> ----------------------------------- #> Main model: #> ----------------------------------- #> Series: estimation_xts[, target_name]  #> Regression with ARIMA(0,0,0) errors  #>  #> Coefficients: #>       intercept    baro #>         -9.3713  0.0982 #> s.e.     1.0730  0.0106 #>  #> sigma^2 = 0.8333:  log likelihood = -98.57 #> AIC=203.14   AICc=203.48   BIC=210.09 #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator models: #> ----------------------------------- #> Series: baro #> Frequency: month (step 1) #> Forecast method: auto.arima #> Series: xts_series  #> ARIMA(1,0,2) with non-zero mean  #>  #> Coefficients: #>          ar1     ma1     ma2      mean #>       0.6688  0.5305  0.3316  100.8580 #> s.e.  0.0653  0.0799  0.0753    1.5774 #>  #> sigma^2 = 18.46:  log likelihood = -646.14 #> AIC=1302.28   AICc=1302.55   BIC=1319.36 #> Aggregation: expalmon #> Estimated expalmon weights: 0.006, 0.994, 0 #> Estimated expalmon parameters: -4.914, -10 #> ----------------------------------- #> Joint expalmon optimization: #> ----------------------------------- #> Method: L-BFGS-B #> Objective value: 60.8316 #> Convergence code: 0 #> Best start: 1 / 3 #> Message: CONVERGENCE: REL_REDUCTION_OF_F <= FACTR*EPSMCH #> -----------------------------------

what: Bridging Data Frequencies for Timely Economic Forecasts
title: Bridging Data Frequencies for Timely Economic Forecasts
text: bridgr designed simplify implementation evaluation bridge models, useful nowcasting (predicting present near-term) forecasting macroeconomic variables like GDP. Bridge models statistical tools link high-frequency indicators (e.g., monthly industrial production) low-frequency target variables (e.g., quarterly GDP) forecasting aggregating indicators match target’s frequency. enable timely predictions official release low-frequency data, making essential policymakers need early insights decision-making. bridgr supports regular frequency ladders second year, several deterministic model-based indicator forecasting rules, multiple aggregation choices including joint expalmon weighting. target period contains high-frequency observations implied current frequency mapping, package keeps recent observations reports summarized warning.

title: Bridging Data Frequencies for Timely Economic Forecasts

title: Bridging Data Frequencies for Timely Economic Forecasts
text: basic example default mean aggregation: want data-driven within-period weights instead simple mean, can switch indicator aggregator expalmon. corresponding weights estimated jointly within bridge model, solver_options let control optimization.
code: if (requireNamespace("bridgr", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(bridgr) } else {   pkgload::load_all(".", export_all = FALSE, helpers = FALSE, quiet = TRUE) } #> Warning in system("timedatectl", intern = TRUE): running command 'timedatectl' #> had status 1  gdp <- suppressMessages(tsbox::ts_na_omit(tsbox::ts_pc(bridgr::gdp)))  bridge_model <- bridge(   target = gdp,    indic = baro,    indic_predict = "auto.arima",    indic_aggregators = "mean",   indic_lags = 2,    target_lags=1,    h=2 ) #> [value]: 'values'  #> [value]: 'values'  forecast(bridge_model) #>    Point Forecast      Lo 80    Hi 80      Lo 95    Hi 95 #> 74      0.8313868 -0.1302710 1.793045 -0.6393418 2.302115 #> 75      0.5363317 -0.4397745 1.512438 -0.9564939 2.029157  summary(bridge_model) #> Bridge model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp #> Target frequency: quarter (step 1) #> Forecast horizon: 2 #> Formula: gdp ~ baro + baro_lag1 + baro_lag2 #> ----------------------------------- #> Main model: #> ----------------------------------- #> Series: estimation_xts[, target_name]  #> Regression with ARIMA(1,0,0) errors  #>  #> Coefficients: #>          ar1  intercept    baro  baro_lag1  baro_lag2 #>       0.1740    -7.4164  0.1574    -0.0957     0.0172 #> s.e.  0.1312     1.4152  0.0126     0.0125     0.0127 #>  #> sigma^2 = 0.5631:  log likelihood = -80.04 #> AIC=172.09   AICc=173.36   BIC=185.83 #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator models: #> ----------------------------------- #> Series: baro #> Frequency: month (step 1) #> Forecast method: auto.arima #> Series: xts_series  #> ARIMA(1,0,2) with non-zero mean  #>  #> Coefficients: #>          ar1     ma1     ma2      mean #>       0.6688  0.5305  0.3316  100.8580 #> s.e.  0.0653  0.0799  0.0753    1.5774 #>  #> sigma^2 = 18.46:  log likelihood = -646.14 #> AIC=1302.28   AICc=1302.55   BIC=1319.36 #> Aggregation: mean #> ----------------------------------- expalmon_model <- bridge(   target = gdp,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "expalmon",   solver_options = list(seed = 123, n_starts = 3),   h = 1 ) #> [value]: 'values'  #> [value]: 'values'  summary(expalmon_model) #> Bridge model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp #> Target frequency: quarter (step 1) #> Forecast horizon: 1 #> Formula: gdp ~ baro #> ----------------------------------- #> Main model: #> ----------------------------------- #> Series: estimation_xts[, target_name]  #> Regression with ARIMA(0,0,0) errors  #>  #> Coefficients: #>       intercept    baro #>         -9.3713  0.0982 #> s.e.     1.0730  0.0106 #>  #> sigma^2 = 0.8333:  log likelihood = -98.57 #> AIC=203.14   AICc=203.48   BIC=210.09 #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator models: #> ----------------------------------- #> Series: baro #> Frequency: month (step 1) #> Forecast method: auto.arima #> Series: xts_series  #> ARIMA(1,0,2) with non-zero mean  #>  #> Coefficients: #>          ar1     ma1     ma2      mean #>       0.6688  0.5305  0.3316  100.8580 #> s.e.  0.0653  0.0799  0.0753    1.5774 #>  #> sigma^2 = 18.46:  log likelihood = -646.14 #> AIC=1302.28   AICc=1302.55   BIC=1319.36 #> Aggregation: expalmon #> Estimated expalmon weights: 0.006, 0.994, 0 #> Estimated expalmon parameters: -4.914, -10 #> ----------------------------------- #> Joint expalmon optimization: #> ----------------------------------- #> Method: L-BFGS-B #> Objective value: 60.8316 #> Convergence code: 0 #> Best start: 1 / 3 #> Message: CONVERGENCE: REL_REDUCTION_OF_F <= FACTR*EPSMCH #> -----------------------------------

title: Estimate a Bridge Model — bridge
text: Estimate bridge model links one lower-frequency target series one higher-frequency indicator series. Indicators aligned target frequency forecasting missing higher-frequency observations aggregating within target period.

title: Estimate a Bridge Model — bridge
code: bridge(   target,   indic,   indic_predict = NULL,   indic_aggregators = NULL,   indic_lags = 0,   target_lags = 0,   h = 1,   frequency_conversions = NULL,   solver_options = NULL,   ... )

title: Estimate a Bridge Model — bridge
text: target single target series tsbox::ts_boxable() format. indic One indicator series tsbox::ts_boxable() format. indic_predict character vector indicator forecasting methods. Length must 1 equal number indicator series. indic_aggregators character vector aggregation methods list numeric weights. Length must 1 equal number indicator series. Numeric weights must sum one appropriate length inferred target-period block size. indic_lags non-negative integer giving number target-period lags add aggregated indicator. target_lags non-negative integer giving autoregressive order target equation. h positive integer forecast horizon measured target periods. frequency_conversions named numeric vector used customize regular frequency ladder. Supported names spm, mph, hpd, dpw, wpm, mpq, qpy. solver_options list optional controls joint expalmon optimization. Supported entries method, maxiter, n_starts, seed, trace. controls ignored unless least one indicator uses indic_aggregators = "expalmon". ... Reserved future extensions.

title: Estimate a Bridge Model — bridge
text: object class "bridge" containing standardized input series, inferred frequencies, aligned estimation forecast datasets, fitted target model, fitted indicator models, metadata required forecast.bridge() summary.bridge().

title: Estimate a Bridge Model — bridge
text: Supported indicator forecasting methods "mean", "last", "auto.arima", "ets". Supported aggregation methods "mean", "last", "sum", "expalmon", numeric weight vector supplied inside list(). one indicators use "expalmon", corresponding aggregation weights estimated jointly final bridge-model objective rather one indicator time. package assumes regular frequency ladder second -> minute -> hour -> day -> week -> month -> quarter -> year. default number lower-level observations per higher-level unit : spm = 60 mph = 60 hpd = 24 dpw = 7 wpm = 4 mpq = 3 qpy = 4 Users can override subset values frequency_conversions. target period contains high-frequency observations implied current mapping, bridge() keeps recent observations emits summarized warning. target period contains fewer observations required, call fails.

title: Estimate a Bridge Model — bridge
text: Baffigi, ., Golinelli, R., & Parigi, G. (2004). Bridge models forecast euro area GDP. International Journal Forecasting, 20(3), 447-460. doi:10.1016/S0169-2070(03)00067-0 Burri, M. (2023). daily lead texts help nowcasting GDP growth? IRENE Working Papers 23-02. https://www5.unine.ch/RePEc/ftp/irn/pdfs/WP23-02.pdf Schumacher, C. (2016). comparison MIDAS bridge equations. International Journal Forecasting, 32(2), 257-270. doi:10.1016/j.ijforecast.2015.07.004

title: Estimate a Bridge Model — bridge
code: gdp_growth <- suppressMessages(tsbox::ts_na_omit(tsbox::ts_pc(gdp)))  model <- bridge(   target = gdp_growth,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "mean",   indic_lags = 1,   target_lags = 1,   h = 2 ) #> [value]: 'values'  #> [value]: 'values'   expalmon_model <- bridge(   target = gdp_growth,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "expalmon",   solver_options = list(seed = 123, n_starts = 3),   h = 1 ) #> [value]: 'values'  #> [value]: 'values'   forecast(model) #>    Point Forecast      Lo 80    Hi 80      Lo 95    Hi 95 #> 75      0.9724040  0.0112021 1.933606 -0.4976274 2.442435 #> 76      0.6984187 -0.2906595 1.687497 -0.8142460 2.211083 summary(expalmon_model) #> Bridge model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Target frequency: quarter (step 1) #> Forecast horizon: 1 #> Formula: gdp_growth ~ baro #> ----------------------------------- #> Main model: #> ----------------------------------- #> Series: estimation_xts[, target_name]  #> Regression with ARIMA(0,0,0) errors  #>  #> Coefficients: #>       intercept    baro #>         -9.3713  0.0982 #> s.e.     1.0730  0.0106 #>  #> sigma^2 = 0.8333:  log likelihood = -98.57 #> AIC=203.14   AICc=203.48   BIC=210.09 #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator models: #> ----------------------------------- #> Series: baro #> Frequency: month (step 1) #> Forecast method: auto.arima #> Series: xts_series  #> ARIMA(1,0,2) with non-zero mean  #>  #> Coefficients: #>          ar1     ma1     ma2      mean #>       0.6688  0.5305  0.3316  100.8580 #> s.e.  0.0653  0.0799  0.0753    1.5774 #>  #> sigma^2 = 18.46:  log likelihood = -646.14 #> AIC=1302.28   AICc=1302.55   BIC=1319.36 #> Aggregation: expalmon #> Estimated expalmon weights: 0.006, 0.994, 0 #> Estimated expalmon parameters: -4.914, -10 #> ----------------------------------- #> Joint expalmon optimization: #> ----------------------------------- #> Method: L-BFGS-B #> Objective value: 60.8316 #> Convergence code: 0 #> Best start: 1 / 3 #> Message: CONVERGENCE: REL_REDUCTION_OF_F <= FACTR*EPSMCH #> title: Swiss Economic Indicators — gdp
text: collection datasets containing economic indicators Switzerland.

title: Swiss Economic Indicators — gdp
text: baro: KOF barometer, monthly business cycle indicator. fcurve: F-curve, daily business cycle indicator. gdp: Quarterly GDP data (real, seasonally adjusted). wea: Weekly Economic Activity (WEA) indicator.

title: Swiss Economic Indicators — gdp
text: baro: Source: KOF Swiss Economic Institute Timeframe: January 2004 - December 2022 Frequency: Monthly Format: tibble monthly observations 2 variables: time: Date, month year observation. values: Numeric, value KOF barometer. fcurve: Source: Burri Kaufmann GitHub Timeframe: January 2004 - December 2022 Frequency: Daily Format: tibble daily observations 2 variables: time: Date, date observation. values: Numeric, value F-curve (inverted compatibility). gdp: Source: SECO Timeframe: January 2004 - December 2022 Frequency: Quarterly Format: tibble quarterly observations 2 variables: time: Date, quarter year observation. values: Numeric, real GDP (seasonally adjusted). wea: Source: SECO WEA Indicator Timeframe: January 2005 - December 2022 Frequency: Weekly Format: tibble weekly observations 2 variables: time: Date, week year observation. values: Numeric, value WEA.

title: bridgr 0.1.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-02-18 Solve dependency issues xts

title: bridgr 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2024-12-13 Added gdp,baro, wea fcurve datasets. Added bridge(), forecast() summary() functions. Supports target variables monthly, quarterly yearly frequency, indicator variables daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly yearly frequency. Supports auto.arima, ets methods indicator variable forecasting. Supports aggregation indicator variables match target’s frequency using custom weighting functions, exponential Almon polynomials methods.
